Footer.description: Contemporary silhouettes, timeless craft. Made by hand in the atelier.
Footer.addressLine: Egzona Abazi Fashion Designer · Prishtinë, Kosovo

Hero.headline: Contemporary silhouettes, timeless craft.
Hero.subtext: A wardrobe drafted by hand, in limited runs — made to outlive the season it was born in.

SignaturePieces.heading: Signature Pieces
SignaturePieces.subtext: A selection from the current édition — cut, draped and finished in the studio this season.
SignaturePieces.comingSoonHeading: The new édition is being finished by hand
SignaturePieces.comingSoonText: Signature pieces will appear here once released. In the meantime, browse the archive or write to the atelier for a private preview.

Wardrobe.heading: Three ways to be dressed by Egzona.

DesignerBio.heading: A single perfect line — that is the whole practice.
DesignerBio.paragraph1: Egzona Abazi is a fashion designer known for elegant, handcrafted dresses that blend timeless silhouettes with modern detail. Every piece is drafted, cut, and finished in the atelier — never sub-contracted, never rushed.
DesignerBio.paragraph2: The result is a wardrobe you can keep for a lifetime: silhouettes that hold their shape, seams you can wear inside-out, fabrics that age the way they should.

Testimonial.quote: “She listened for an hour before she drew a single line. What arrived twelve weeks later was the dress I didn't know I'd been describing my whole life.”

intro.heading: Egzona Abazi
intro.paragraph1: Egzona Abazi is a fashion designer known for elegant, handcrafted dresses that blend timeless silhouettes with modern detail. Every piece in the collection is designed with meticulous attention to fabric, fit, and finish.
intro.paragraph2: From evening gowns to bridal pieces, each dress is created to be worn for life's most memorable occasions. Browse the collections to find a piece that speaks to you, and reach out with any questions — the team is happy to help with sizing, fabric, and availability.

manifesto.quote: “A dress should not announce itself. It should hold the woman inside it — quietly, precisely — and then disappear.”

principles.heading: How the atelier works
principles.item1Text: We draw the silhouette until it is right on the body. Everything else is added last, or not at all.
principles.item2Text: No piece is produced in more than a handful. Each garment is recorded in the atelier archive.
principles.item3Text: Silks, wools and laces chosen for how they behave in ten years, not ten minutes.
principles.item4Text: Every hem, every buttonhole, every bead. If it can be done by hand, it is.

craft.heading: Hands, thread, hours.
craft.text: A single evening dress takes between 60 and 200 hours in the atelier. Beading, hand-rolled hems and silk linings account for most of them.
craft.imageAlt: A dress finished in the atelier

house.heading: A short history
house.m1Text: Studies in fashion design between Prishtinë and Milan.
house.m2Text: First private commissions — evening and bridal, by referral only.
house.m3Text: The debut ready-to-wear capsule, presented in a single evening.
house.m4Text: Introduction of made-to-measure with a three-fitting protocol.
house.m5Text: Sold internationally, still finished by the same hands.

appointments.text: Fittings by appointment at the atelier in Prishtinë, and on request while traveling.
